name: Anas ibn al-Harith
title: Companion of the Prophet
shortDescription: An elderly and venerable companion of the Prophet (PBUH) who had heard the hadith of Hussain's (AS) martyrdom from the Messenger of God.
fullStory:
Anas ibn al-Harith al-Kahili is one of the few martyrs of Karbala who had the honor of meeting the Prophet of Islam (PBUH) and participating in the battles of Badr and Hunayn. He was an elderly and venerable companion who lived among the people of Kufa for years with a wealth of memories of the Messenger of God (PBUH).

Anas ibn al-Harith was himself the narrator of one of the most famous hadiths of the Prophet (PBUH) regarding the tragedy of Ashura. He narrated that one day the Messenger of God (PBUH), holding Hussain (AS) in his arms, said: 'This son of mine will be killed in a land called Karbala; whoever among you is present on that day must help him'. Anas had kept this hadith in his heart for years.

When Imam Hussain (AS) reached Karbala, Anas, who was now a frail old man with a bent back, secretly left Kufa at night and joined the Imam's caravan to fulfill the Prophet's (PBUH) command. The presence of this elderly companion among the Imam's supporters left no excuse for many Kufans.

On the day of Ashura, Anas asked the Imam for permission to go to the battlefield. Due to his old age, his eyebrows drooped over his eyes; he tied his eyebrows to his forehead with a cloth so he could see the enemy. Imam Hussain (AS), seeing this scene, tearfully said: 'May God thank you, O old man'.

Anas entered the battlefield with a bent posture but a firm spirit and recited war poetry with a trembling voice. Despite his old age, he fought remarkably and killed 18 enemy soldiers until he finally achieved his long-held wish and was martyred alongside the son of the Prophet (PBUH).
quote: (Imam Hussain, upon seeing Anas tie his eyebrows:) May God thank you, O old man.
